key_insight: A neural-network-based time integrator can replace costly plasticity state updates while preserving the plastic consistency condition and delivering accurate, stable results.
engineering_relevance: This approach can reduce constitutive-model computation time in finite element simulations of nonlinear inelastic structures, enabling larger time steps with lower cost.
method: Train neural networks to predict internal state variable updates directly from invariants.
Determine evolution directions analytically from the training data.
Enforce exact plastic consistency in a von Mises plasticity model with nonlinear kinematic hardening.
evidence_signal: The framework was trained using only 10 cycles of multiaxial proportional loading and evaluated with both material point simulations and finite element boundary value problems, showing very good accuracy, numerical stability, and noticeable speedup for a single strain increment per load segment.
watch_item: Performance is demonstrated on a prototype plasticity model, so robustness and generalization to more complex materials, loading paths, and broader FE settings still need careful validation.
paper_title: Machine Learning-Accelerated Time Integration of Plasticity Models
